channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: What The Wall Street Journal Gets Wrong About The Talking Filibuster
item.description: The talking filibuster has been a tool in the Senate’s arsenal for 200 years. Returning to it could unlock the majesty of the institution.

item.title: While big tech burns cash on AI, Apple waits
item.description: The company's apparent absence from the AI race may be its most disciplined strategic decision in years.

item.title: Trump withholds endorsement in fiery GOP Senate primary as early voting begins in Texas
item.description: Trump remains undecided on the Texas Senate primary as Cornyn faces MAGA challengers Paxton and Hunt in a competitive Republican race ahead of March 3...

item.title: Trump urges Iran, Cuba to make deals -- or else
item.description: President Trump is urging countries on two sides of the globe -- Iran and Cuba -- to make deals with the U.S. or suffer grave consequences.

item.title: Georgia AG Chris Carr refuses to make taxpayers foot bill for prison inmates' sex-change surgeries
item.description: The state of Georgia is fighting in federal court to enforce its law banning prisoners from sex-change surgeries funded by taxpayers, and the state's ...

item.title: Trump task force racks up 500 arrests in January as president brands cartels ‘ISIS of the Western Hemisphere’
item.description: Trump's Homeland Security Task Force reportedly arrested over 500 people in January surge targeting cartels, gangs, and smuggling rings across the nat...

item.title: America Seeks Partner, Europe Swipes Left
item.description: There is a kind of romantic streak to U.S. relations with Europe in the Trump era.

item.title: Congress Should Close the Big Hospitals Tax Loophole
item.description: Large nonprofit hospital networks are leveraging huge subsidies for financial gains and political advantage.

item.title: Colbert defies CBS, airs banned James Talarico interview on YouTube
item.description: Late-night host Stephen Colbert openly defied his own network Monday evening, revealing that CBS lawyers barred him from interviewing Texas Democratic...

item.title: The Rev. Jesse Jackson, a civil rights icon, has died at 84
item.description: The Rev. Jesse Jackson, who grew up in Jim Crow South Carolina and went on to become a civil rights icon and the first Black male candidate for presid...

item.title: Stop Another Major Medicaid Money-Laundering Scam Before It’s Too Late
item.description: States are abusing intergovernmental transfers at patients’ and taxpayers’ expense.

item.title: Trump Admin Fights To Keep Illegal Immigrants From Distorting Census
item.description: The Census Bureau recently announced its plan to administer a survey that includes the query 'Is this person a citizen of the United States?'

item.title: D-Wave CEO shrugs off short attacks with ‘revolutionary’ $550 million quantum computing acquisition
item.description: Less than 10% of D-Wave's clients are government research contracts, Alan Baratz says, proof it is offering commercially viable services.

item.title: Trump hammers AOC Munich stumbles as 'not a good look for the United States'
item.description: Trump criticized Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez's Munich Security Conference performance, calling it "not a good look for the United States" on foreign...

item.title: Election integrity groups press Supreme Court to require ballots by Election Day
item.description: Election integrity groups urge Supreme Court to ban counting mail-in ballots arriving after Election Day, potentially affecting laws in 14 states and ...

item.title: My Students Protesting ICE Cannot Answer One Question On The U.S. Citizenship Exam
item.description: It is time to restore education's core mission: knowledge first, civics always, activism never.

item.title: How Oklahomans Can Outsmart The Bureaucracy To Require Better History Instruction In Public Schools
item.description: Knowledge is not an optional decoration in a democracy. It is the raw material of self-government.

item.title: For success in AI, avoid the ‘efficiency trap’— and focus on trust instead
item.description: Trust can make or break success in AI implementation.

item.title: AOC’s Risible Performance
item.description: AOC’s time at the Munich conference was another reminder that no matter how much she is billed as a rising star, she is still callow and unserious.

item.title: Rubio’s Impressive Tightrope Walk
item.description: The secretary of state stayed true to President Trump while reassuring Europe.

item.title: Two More Trans Killers Carry Out Atrocities
item.description: There are dots here that should be connected.

item.title: Anderson Cooper says goodbye to CBS, staying with CNN; will leave '60 Minutes' after two decades
item.description: Anderson Cooper, who has reported for CBS' "60 Minutes" for the past two decades in addition to hosting a weeknight news program on CNN, said Monday t...

item.title: AI investments surge in India as tech leaders convene for Delhi summit
item.description: Blackstone, AMD, and Tata Consultancy Services unveil major infrastructure plans as OpenAI and Anthropic focus on India growth.

item.title: As boomer and Gen X bosses retire, working from home will make a major comeback, new research predicts
item.description: A new study of 8,000 workers confirms that Gen Z CEOs are set to bring back working from home as they take over—turning today’s office mandates into a...

item.title: Texas early voting begins ahead of contentious March 3 primary elections
item.description: Texas primary voters may begin early voting Tuesday for the March 3 primary elections, though the races could advance to a runoff if no candidate secu...

item.title: Evolving Your Wealth Management Practice for 2026 and Beyond
item.description: Wealth management’s 2026 outlook: growth will hinge on transparency, integration, and relevance to women and next-generation investors. The post Evolv...

item.title: AI Is Reshaping Bank Risk
item.description: AI is exposing the limits of legacy bank controls, making governance quality critical to resilience and investor confidence. The post AI Is Reshaping ...

item.title: What Makes an Ideal Leveraged Buyout Candidate?
item.description: In competitive PE markets, successful LBOs depend on durable business models, recurring cash flows, defensible market positions, and disciplined targe...

item.title: Rethinking Household Asset Allocation Under Capital Constraints
item.description: Leveraged ETFs aren’t about more risk, they’re about using less capital to achieve a desired risk profile in household portfolios. The post Rethinking...

item.title: Stockholm’s Capital Markets Success: More Than Meatballs
item.description: Stockholm’s capital-market success reflects more than IPOs and PE activity. It offers a case study in how culture, institutions, and incentives shape ...
